Class EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E extends IEfsEvent>

java.lang.Object
org.efs.bus.EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E>
Type Parameters:
E - event class
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
Enclosing class:
EfsEventBus
public static final class EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E extends IEfsEvent> extends Object
An advertisement is an publishing agent's access point to a event class+topic feed. Publisher uses this advertisement to:
  1. Set the publisher's event publishing status. Setting this value to true means that the fowardEvent is able to fowardEvent events (although is may chose not to do so) and false means that is is unable to fowardEvent events (and is not allowed to do so).
  2. Publish events to extant subscribers. Doing so requires this advertisement to be 1) open, 2) fowardEvent status is true, and 3) there are subscribers to this topic.
Once an advertisement is closed, it cannot be re-opened and setting fowardEvent status or publishing events is disallowed. Event count and latest event timestamp may still be retrieved but these values will never change once the advertisement is closed. That said, hasSubscribers() will reflect the current topic subscription status since that is independent of advertisements.
Author:
Charles W. Rapp

    Set to true if access point is active and false otherwise.

    Timestamp of latest event posted/received on this topic.

    Set to true if there is a publisher actively posting events to this topic and false if not.

    void
    close()
    Closes this advertisement.
    final long

    Returns total number of events posted/received by this advertisement/subscription since opening.

    Returns true if there is at least one subscriber to this topic and false if there are none.
    final boolean

    Returns true if this access point is open and false if closed.
    final boolean

    Returned value interpretation depends on whether this is an advertisement or a subscription: Advertisement: true means that publisher is able to post events to topic and false if unable to post.

    Returns timestamp of latest event posted/received by this advertisement/subscription.
    void

    Publishes given event to all extant subscribers.

    Sets publisher's active publishing status to given value where true means publisher can post event and false means publisher cannot do so.

    Returns event class+topic key for this feed.

  • Field Details

    • mTopic

      protected final org.efs.bus.EfsEventBus.TopicFeed<E extends IEfsEvent> mTopic
      Accesses this topic feed.

    • mAgent

      protected final IEfsAgent mAgent
      Publishing or subscribing agent. Set to null for a RouterSubscription.

    • mActive

      protected final AtomicBoolean mActive
      Set to true if access point is active and false otherwise. Initially set to true.

    • mPubStatus

      protected final AtomicBoolean mPubStatus
      Set to true if there is a publisher actively posting events to this topic and false if not. Initially set to false.

    • mEventCount

      protected long mEventCount
      Total number of events posted/received on this advertisement/subscription.

    • mLatestEvent

      protected Instant mLatestEvent
      Timestamp of latest event posted/received on this topic. Initialized to epoch time.

  • Method Details

    • close

      public void close() throws Exception
      Closes this advertisement. If will no longer be possible to publish events to this advertisement after this point.

      If advertisement is already closed, then does nothing.

      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs closing this advertisement.

    • hasSubscribers

      public boolean hasSubscribers()
      Returns true if there is at least one subscriber to this topic and false if there are none. publish(IEfsEvent) may be called only when this method returns true.
      Returns:
      true if publisher has subscribers and is clear to fowardEvent events to this advertisement.

    • publishStatus

      public void publishStatus(boolean pubStatus)
      Sets publisher's active publishing status to given value where true means publisher can post event and false means publisher cannot do so. If given publishing status is different than current status, then all extant subscribers are informed of this change.
      Parameters:
      pubStatus - active publishing status.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this advertisement is closed.

    • publish

      public void publish(E event)
      Publishes given event to all extant subscribers.
      Parameters:
      event - forward this event to subscribers.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if event is null.
      IllegalStateException - if:
      • if this advertisement is closed or
      • if publishing agent has not set publish status to true.

    • topicKey

      public final EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey()
      Returns event class+topic key for this feed.
      Returns:
      event class+topic key.

    • isOpen

      public final boolean isOpen()
      Returns true if this access point is open and false if closed. Note: once an access point is closed, it remains closed and cannot be re-opened.
      Returns:
      true if open and false if closed.

    • isPublished

      public final boolean isPublished()
      Returned value interpretation depends on whether this is an advertisement or a subscription:
      • Advertisement: true means that publisher is able to post events to topic and false if unable to post.
      • Subscription: true means there is at least one publisher actively posting events to this topic and false means there are no publishers.
      Note: a true value does not mean that events are actively being posted to the topic but only that they may be posted. Conversely, false means that no events are being posted to the topic.
      Returns:
      true if events may be posted to topic and false if events are not being posted to topic.

    • eventCount

      public final long eventCount()
      Returns total number of events posted/received by this advertisement/subscription since opening.
      Returns:
      total number of events on this access point.

    • latestEvent

      @Nonnull public final Instant latestEvent()
      Returns timestamp of latest event posted/received by this advertisement/subscription. If no event has crossed this access point, then returns epoch time.
      Returns:
      latest event timestamp.
